name: refac-10-error-handling-unification
initialPrompt: Error handling in our Python microservices is completely inconsistent — some services return RFC 7807 Problem Details, some return custom JSON blobs, and three services just let unhandled exceptions bubble up to a 500 with a stack trace in the body. Our mobile app has to special-case every service's error format. I want: a unified error response standard across all 12 services, a shared exception hierarchy in our internal PyPI package, and a plan to retrofit all existing endpoints without breaking clients.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: Error handling unification across 12 Python microservices

blocks.text: Unify error responses across all 12 Python microservices using RFC 7807 Problem Details
successCriteria: All endpoints return RFC 7807 compliant errors
Mobile client removes all service-specific error handling
Zero stack traces exposed in API responses

blocks.title: Audit current error formats and unhandled exception paths across all 12 services
acceptance: Error format inventory per service per endpoint category
All unhandled exception paths listed

blocks.title: Design shared exception hierarchy in internal PyPI package
acceptance: Base exception classes published to internal PyPI
Each error category maps to an RFC 7807 type URI

blocks.title: Implement RFC 7807 error middleware for FastAPI and Flask
acceptance: Middleware converts all exceptions to RFC 7807 format
Stack traces suppressed in non-debug mode

blocks.title: Retrofit all 12 services with shared exception hierarchy and middleware
acceptance: All services use shared exception hierarchy
Client compatibility tests pass for each service

blocks.question: Adopt RFC 7807 Problem Details or design a custom error envelope?
blocks.rationale: Industry standard; clients can use generic RFC 7807 parsers.

blocks.question: Can the mobile client ship a backward-compatible update before we change the error format, or do we need a versioned error endpoint?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same decision about RFC 7807 vs custom envelope, but id changed from dec-error-standard to dec-rfc7807-adoption. AC-13 re-anchoring must map comments from dec-error-standard to dec-rfc7807-adoption.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'RFC 7807', 'middleware', and 'error' with task-middleware. A comment on task-middleware must stay on task-middleware, not migrate to task-sentry-integration.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-client-compat-suite inserted at position 1 shifts all subsequent tasks; task-sentry-integration inserted before dec block; dec-error-standard renamed to dec-rfc7807-adoption; oq-client-migration renamed to oq-mobile-rollout. expectedPreservedIds has 5 entries, excluding the changed ids and genuinely new blocks.
